feedback.text: A lot of exploitation comes from companies 'lending' or 'licensing' use of their software and/or media. As it stands, this act covers too little and may even be outdated by the time it has been passed. For this act to succeed, I suggest diving deeper into what things people frequently do to obtain digital ownership, despite a company not wanting you to. Things such as: -Why do people bypass restrictions? (piracy, exploits, etc) -Why do people preserve older versions of a given game or software? -Why do people circumvent malicious advertisements by site owners? (Such as Youtube advertising gambling, content farms or other content that may exploit them).
